title: Castle in the Air - Chapter Two: In which Abdullah is mistaken for a young lady (Fragment 2)
text:
“Are you sure?” she asked. “You are wearing a dress.”

Abdullah looked down and discovered that, in the way of dreams, he was wearing his nightshirt. “This is just my strange foreign garb,” he said hastily. “My true country is far from here. I assure you that I am a man.”

“Oh, no,” she said decidedly. “You can’t be a man. You’re quite the wrong shape. Men are twice as thick as you all over, and their stomachs come out in a fat bit that’s called a belly. And they have gray hair all over their faces and nothing but shiny skin on their heads. You’ve got hair on your head like me and almost none on your face.” Then, as Abdullah put his hand rather indignantly to the six hairs on his upper lip, she asked, “Or have you got bare skin under your hat?”

“Certainly not,” said Abdullah, who was proud of his thick, wavy hair. He put his hand to his head and removed what turned out to be his nightcap. “Look,” he said.

“Ah,” she said. Her lovely face was puzzled. “You have hair that’s almost as nice as mine. I don’t understand.”

“I’m not sure I do, either,” said Abdullah. “Could it be that you have not seen very many men?”

“Of course not,” she said. “Don’t be silly. I’ve only seen my father! But I’ve seen quite a lot of him, so I do know.”

“But don’t you ever go out at all?” Abdullah asked helplessly.

She laughed. “Yes, I’m out now. This is my night garden. My father had it made so that I wouldn’t ruin my looks going out in the sun.”

“I mean, out into the town, to see all the people,” Abdullah explained.

“Well, no, not yet,” she admitted. As if that bothered her a little, she twirled away from him and went to sit on the edge of the fountain. Turning to look up at him, she said, “My father tells me I might be able to go out and see the town sometimes after I’m married—if my husband allows me to—but it won’t be this town. My father’s arranging for me to marry a prince from Ochinstan. Until then I have to stay inside these walls, of course.”

Abdullah had heard that some of the very rich people in Zanzib kept their daughters—and even their wives, too—almost like prisoners inside their grand houses. He had many times wished someone would keep his father’s first wife’s sister, Fatima, that way. But now, in this dream, it seemed to him that this custom was entirely unreasonable and not fair to this lovely girl at all. Fancy not knowing what a normal young man looked like!

“Pardon my asking, but is the Prince from Ochinstan perhaps old and a little ugly?” he said.

“Well,” she said, evidently not quite sure, “my father says he’s in his prime, just as my father is himself. But I believe the problem lies in the brutal nature of men. If another man saw me before the Prince did, my father says he would instantly fall in love with me and carry me off, which would ruin all my father’s plans, naturally. He says most men are great beasts. Are you a beast?”

meta.author: Diana Wynne Jones
meta.sectionTitle: Chapter Two: In which Abdullah is mistaken for a young lady

vocabulary.definition: Planning or preparing something in advance.
vocabulary.example: My father’s arranging for me to marry a prince from Ochinstan.

vocabulary.definition: Savage and cruel.
vocabulary.example: I believe the problem lies in the brutal nature of men.

vocabulary.definition: To abduct or take someone away by force.
vocabulary.example: He would instantly fall in love with me and carry me off, which would ruin all my father’s plans.

vocabulary.definition: A traditional practice or usual way of doing something in a particular society or place.
vocabulary.example: This custom was entirely unreasonable and not fair to this lovely girl at all.

vocabulary.definition: In a clear and definite way; unquestionably.
vocabulary.example: Oh, no, she said decidedly.

vocabulary.definition: Meaningful multi-word expressions and collocations such as 'twirled away', 'carry me off', 'ruin my looks'.
vocabulary.example: She twirled away from him; would instantly fall in love with me and carry me off; ruin my looks going out in the sun.

vocabulary.definition: To imagine or think something, sometimes incorrectly.
vocabulary.example: Fancy not knowing what a normal young man looked like!

vocabulary.definition: Clothing or dress, especially of a distinctive or special kind.
vocabulary.example: This is just my strange foreign garb.

vocabulary.definition: In a hurried or rushed manner.
vocabulary.example: My true country is far from here. I assure you that I am a man, he said hastily.

vocabulary.definition: In a manner that shows inability to do anything to improve a situation.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah asked helplessly.

vocabulary.definition: In a manner expressing anger or annoyance at something perceived as unfair.
vocabulary.example: As Abdullah put his hand rather indignantly to the six hairs on his upper lip.

vocabulary.definition: At once; immediately.
vocabulary.example: If another man saw me before the Prince did, my father says he would instantly fall in love with me.

vocabulary.definition: A soft cap worn in bed to keep the head warm.
vocabulary.example: He removed what turned out to be his nightcap.

vocabulary.definition: Conforming to the usual, typical, or expected standard.
vocabulary.example: Fancy not knowing what a normal young man looked like!

vocabulary.definition: Decisions about what one intends to do in the future.
vocabulary.example: That would ruin all my father’s plans.

vocabulary.definition: The best, most successful, or most productive stage.
vocabulary.example: He’s in his prime, just as my father is himself.

vocabulary.definition: People kept in captivity or confined, especially legally.
vocabulary.example: Some of the very rich people kept their daughters—and even their wives—almost like prisoners inside their grand houses.

vocabulary.definition: Confused or perplexed.
vocabulary.example: Her lovely face was puzzled.

vocabulary.definition: To spoil or destroy the condition or quality of something.
vocabulary.example: My father had it made so that I wouldn’t ruin my looks going out in the sun.

vocabulary.definition: Turned or spun quickly and moved away.
vocabulary.example: She twirled away from him and went to sit on the edge of the fountain.

vocabulary.definition: Not guided by or based on good sense.
vocabulary.example: It seemed to him that this custom was entirely unreasonable and not fair to this lovely girl at all.

vocabulary.definition: Desired something to happen or be the case.
vocabulary.example: He had many times wished someone would keep his father’s first wife’s sister, Fatima, that way.

vocabulary.definition: Having lived or existed for only a short time.
vocabulary.example: Chapter Two: In which Abdullah is mistaken for a young lady.
